name: Restore The Clean Compact PTX Anchor After The Failed Three-CTA Probe

summary.name: Restore The Clean Compact PTX Anchor After The Failed Three-CTA Probe
summary.hypothesis: The round-19 loss shows that forcing 3-CTA residency hurts the compact PTX issue schedule more than it helps latency hiding. Restoring `__launch_bounds__(128, 2)` should return the branch to the clean compact anchor and recover the 1.39 ms regression.
summary.expected_bottleneck: The immediate problem is a failed register-budget probe, not an unresolved algorithmic bottleneck.
summary.risk: Low. This is a one-line rollback to the known-good clean anchor configuration.
ranking_notes: This is the only rational top choice after the 3-CTA probe produced a large regression with clear causal evidence.
It restores the best known current branch surface without reopening any speculative family on the last round.
Frontier should prefer this over all stale open candidates because the branch is in a known-lost state that needs direct recovery.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if restoring the clean anchor requires anything beyond resetting the PTX microkernel launch-bounds annotation from 3 back to 2.
expected_local_changes: Reset the compact PTX microkernel launch-bounds annotation from 3 back to 2.
Leave grouped_rows=4, the clean wait/sync cadence, dispatch routing, and stage depth unchanged.

notes: Node C may now implement this one candidate.
